Show / Hide Table of Contents

Struct InputEditorId

Represents a logical identifier for an input editor type.

Implements
IEquatable<InputEditorId>
Inherited Members
object.Equals(object, object)
object.ReferenceEquals(object, object)
object.GetType()
Namespace: PhiRobotics.UI.Editors
Assembly: PhiRobotics.UI.dll
Syntax
public readonly struct InputEditorId : IEquatable<InputEditorId>
Remarks

InputEditorId is a lightweight value object used to uniquely identify a specific type of input editor (for example, text, boolean, numeric, or date editors).

It is primarily used with IInputEditorFactory to request the creation of a specific editor type via Create(InputEditorId).

Predefined identifiers are provided for common editor types.
Examples

The following example demonstrates how to create an editor using an identifier:

IInputEditorFactory factory = GetFactory();

IInputEditor editor = factory.Create(InputEditorId.TextEdit);

Constructors

InputEditorId(string)

Initializes a new instance of the InputEditorId struct with the specified logical name.

Declaration
public InputEditorId(string name)
Parameters
Type Name Description
string name

The logical name that uniquely identifies the input editor type.
Remarks

Custom editor identifiers can be created using this constructor.

It is recommended to use a descriptive name to avoid conflicts with other identifiers.

Exceptions
Type Condition
ArgumentException

Thrown if name is null, empty, or consists only of whitespace.

Identifies a boolean (true/false) input editor.

TimeSpanEdit
